title: Chattr session template
description: Design-time document for SessionStore / SessionEngine. Normative server validation: session_store.validate_session_template() (app.py uses this for drafts). This schema matches that function's rules; some rules (participants ⊆ roles) are described here and MUST be enforced by tooling or the Python validator — pure JSON Schema cannot relate phase.participants[] to root.roles[] without implementation-specific extensions.

id.description: Stable library key (e.g. planning). Loaded from filename stem if omitted in file. Required for custom_templates persistence.

name.description: Display name. Python rejects falsy/missing (empty string fails); whitespace-only strings are currently accepted by validate_session_template — avoid them in authored templates.

description.description: Human-readable summary; not validated by validate_session_template. Present on shipped templates.

is_custom.description: Set when saved via /api/sessions/save-draft or draft start path. Ignored by validation.

user_required.description: Shipped templates may include this; engine does not read it — documentation / UI hint only unless you wire it.

roles.description: Abstract role ids (1–6). Used as keys in runtime `cast`. Each cast value MUST be the canonical chat message `sender` string that will post when it is that role’s turn (registered agent instance name or human username), matching SessionEngine’s sender == expected_agent check (see session_engine._get_expected_agent / cast). Order of entries in this array is not significant for the engine; duplicates are allowed by Python but should be avoided in new templates.

items.title: Role id
items.description: Referenced by phase.participants[]. Must match exactly (string equality) entries in roles[].

phases.description: Sequential stages. Engine runs phases in array order (index 0 first). Within a phase, participants[] defines strict turn order.

phase.description: One phase. Unknown keys (e.g. turn_order) are allowed by Python and ignored by validate_session_template.

name.description: Phase label. Python uses truthiness: missing or empty string fails.

participants.description: Ordered list of role ids for this phase (1–4 entries). Order = turn order. Each element MUST be one of the strings in root.roles (not expressible as standard JSON Schema).

prompt.description: Injected into agent/human instruction for this phase. If omitted, engine treats as empty string. Python only enforces max length when value is a str; keep JSON string.

is_output.description: Exactly one phase in the template must be true. When the final phase completes, if this phase is the last phase and is_output is true, session_store stores output_message_id from the completing chat message.

turn_order.description: Shipped templates use "sequential"; engine ignores — turn order is solely participants[] order.
